weaponLimit.ruleText: A survivor may equip at most 2 weapon items. Those weapons may have the same name (for example, two Katars) or two different names. The number of distinct equipped weapon names can never exceed 2.

armourSetBonusRule.completePiecesRequired: All listed pieces for that set
armourSetBonusRule.specialAbilityCard: Injected into the survivor's deck only while the complete set remains equipped; removed immediately when the set is broken.
armourSetBonusRule.stacking: A survivor may benefit from multiple sets only if equipment names/pieces satisfy each set and equipment slot rules allow it.

universalTargetingRule: No equipment card effect may require fighting its source monster/quarry. Source fields control unlock/crafting flavour only.

colorAffinityRule.stacking: Affinities stack independently from armour set bonuses. A single item may count for exactly one colour affinity and one armour set if eligible.
colorAffinityRule.dynamicCards: At 5 matching affinity pieces, add the matching affinity ability card to the survivor deck; remove it immediately when the count falls below 5.

terminologyRule.canonicalSource: GitHub repo samuelkenworthy91-byte/KDM src/data/quarries.js, src/data/resources.js, src/data/equipment.js, src/data/innovationCards.js
terminologyRule.ruleText: Game-facing gear data must use repo quarry IDs, quarry display names, crafting building IDs/display names and resource IDs.

armourBodySlotRule.ruleText: Armour pieces occupy exactly one body slot. A survivor can equip only one Head, Chest, Waist, Arms and Legs armour item.

jewellerySlotRule.ruleText: Charms, circlets, rings, amulets, badges, earrings and similar items use the single Jewellery slot.

utilitySlotRule.ruleText: Utility gear fills remaining space after the 9-piece cap and named slot limits are checked.

weaponStrategyRule.ruleText: Every weapon has a weaponStrategyId that describes its intended survivor deck plan and ramp/payoff pattern.

uncappedScalingRule.ruleText: Damage/block/reward scaling effects should not include arbitrary 'to a max of' caps; difficult ramping is allowed to reach big numbers.

cardCopyRule.ruleText: Each equipped item instance contributes 1 copy of each of its cards by default. A survivor can train a specific card from that specific item instance to add extra copies, up to 3 total copies of that card from that one item.
cardCopyRule.duplicateItemRule: Duplicate items are tracked separately by gearInstanceId. Two identical weapons can each train their own copies, but each item instance is still capped at 3 copies of a given card.
cardCopyRule.unequipRule: Extra copies granted by an item are removed from the deck immediately when that item is unequipped, destroyed, or no longer valid.

progressionSuggestion.copy2: Unlock the second copy after 3 successful plays of that card while the item is equipped, then pay 1 matching resource or 1 generic resource.
progressionSuggestion.copy3: Unlock the third copy after 6 total successful plays of that card while the item is equipped, then pay 1 rare/matching quarry resource or 2 generic resources.
progressionSuggestion.successfulPlayDefinition: The card resolves at least one meaningful effect: damage, block, healing, draw/filtering, status application, survival gain, resource gain, or party support.

cardCopyRule.implementationReminder: Do not duplicate rows in the static card table for trained copies. Build the runtime deck by expanding cards according to each survivor's gearCardTraining state.
